We're looking at Eva Swoboda, a great young talent at the moment. It's great to see you here. How much are you enjoying these World Relays so far? I am having a ball. Just talk me through what these moments used to be like for you as you were getting ready to race. The crowd is getting all excited. The PA is going around. Are in zoning out what's happening well you know the crowd gets you excited because you want to get out there and you want to perform well because they want to see a good performance and you know depending on what leg you're at first leg is always like okay i gotta make i gotta get out the blocks i gotta set the relay off to a good you know a good pace and for me i was either second or anchor so for me it was about getting my mark and making sure I give a good target that's the main thing get that good target don't look behind don't stop get the baton and just go just how much does your training come in because in the in the all the cut and thrust of a four by one in particular it must be very easy for it all to just go away at the last minute. Well, you know, training is important. It's hard when, like for the U.S., I know we didn't get together a lot. Like you have the Polish team, the Russian team. Those teams actually work together a lot on their handoffs. We get together and it could be different people at different times.
